title: The Krieg Pirates Bring War to the Baratie
description: On pages 1–4, Don Krieg’s arrival turns the Baratie crisis into a pirate siege while Nami’s departure and the looming fight split the crew’s attention.
scene: The Baratie becomes trapped between hungry enemies, terrified cooks, Krieg’s overwhelming force, and the Straw Hats’ personal priorities.

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: So you were alive, red-leg Zeff...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: The peerless captain and cook of his own pirate crew.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: So what if I'm alive?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It's got nothing to do with you.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: As you can see, I'm living as a mere cook now.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Hahaha... interesting to hear it put that way...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But from the way I see it, you're living as a cook not by choice,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But because that's the only thing you can do.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: with the way you are now, you can't wear your infamous "Red Shoes."

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: To speak of red-leg Zeff,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Is to speak of the Master of Kicking who refused to use his hands in Battle!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The pirate whose destructive kicks could pulverize Bedrock,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: And leave a clear imprint even on steel!

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: Your nickname "Red-leg" refers to your legs,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: that would be died in the blood of your enemies after a fight.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Though I've heard from rumours that you died during a shipwreck,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It seems that you've managed to survive, though at the cost of one of your precious legs.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: With one of your legs gone, you shouldn't be able to fight anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I may not be able to fight anymore but I can still cook as long as I have these hands.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But just what are you getting at exactly?

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: Red-leg Zeff, you're a man who once braved the waters of the devil's hideout, the grand line itself!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Not only that, you returned without injury.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You must've kept a log during your time in Grand Line. So what I'm getting at is...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Hand over that log to me!

dialogue.speaker: Zeff
dialogue.text: Cool, you've been to grand line, old man?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You are certainly correct to assume that I kept a log, and I indeed have it to this day.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But I shan't hand that over to you.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: That log is the pride of me and my men who bravely ventured grand line together.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: It's much too valuable to give to the likes of you!

summary: This part escalates the Baratie arc from character introduction to open conflict, with Krieg threatening the restaurant and Nami pulling the story toward Arlong Park.
